_comment: Settings for outreach.js (daily sender) and discover.js (candidate finder). Secrets come from env vars / GitHub secrets, NOT this file.
_dailyCap: Emails per run. Workflow runs every 4 hours (6x/day) = ~120 emails/day total.
_dryRun: TRUE = generate and print the emails but send NOTHING. Review at least one dry run, then set false. The DRY_RUN env var overrides this.
_weekdaysOnly: Skip Saturday/Sunday runs — professors read email on weekdays.
_gmailLabel: Gmail label applied to every sent email (created automatically). Set the same value as `gmailLabel` in config.json if you want professor-followup to chase ONLY these threads.
_candidateSource: Use googleSheets to read the Candidates tab, or csv to read candidates.csv.
_sheetName: A separate tab used by automation. The existing Outreach Tracker tab is not modified.
_model: Gemini model that writes the personalized emails.
_delaySecondsBetweenEmails: Base pause between sends (randomized ±30%) so the batch doesn't look like a blast.
_resumePath: Path to your resume PDF (relative to the repo, so GitHub Actions can find it too) — attached to every outreach email. Set to "" to send without an attachment.
_skipAddresses: Specific addresses to never email, e.g. someone you already reached another way.

discover._areas: Area names: ai (AAAI/IJCAI), vision (CVPR/ICCV/ECCV), mlmining (ICML/ICLR/NeurIPS/KDD), nlp (ACL/EMNLP/NAACL), inforet (SIGIR/WWW), robotics (ICRA/IROS/RSS). Raw CSRankings conference codes also work, e.g. "iclr".
discover._excludeCountries: Two-letter CSRankings country codes to exclude. ["in"] = anywhere abroad (everything except India). US institutions have no code and are always included unless you filter differently.
discover._sinceYear: Only count publications from this year onward when ranking professors by activity — keeps the list to people actively publishing.
discover._limit: How many top-ranked professors to put in candidates.csv.
